name: Mundaka Upanishad
summary: The Mundaka Upanishad distinguishes between the lower knowledge (apara vidya — sciences, rituals) and the higher knowledge (para vidya — knowledge of Brahman). It teaches that Brahman is the source of all, like sparks from fire, and can only be known through a qualified teacher.

verses.sanskrit: द्वे विद्ये वेदितव्ये इति ह स्म यद्ब्रह्मविदो वदन्ति परा चैवापरा च ||१.१.४||
verses.transliteration: dve vidye veditavye iti ha sma yad brahmavido vadanti parā caivāparā ca ||1.1.4||
verses.english_translation: Two kinds of knowledge are to be known — so say the knowers of Brahman — the higher and the lower.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: परा — ब्रह्मज्ञानम्। अपरा — ऋग्वेदादिचतुर्वेदाः सशिक्षाव्याकरणादयश्च।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The higher knowledge is the direct knowledge of Brahman. The lower knowledge comprises the four Vedas along with their six auxiliaries — phonetics, ritual, grammar, etymology, metre, and astronomy.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रापरा ऋग्वेदो यजुर्वेदः सामवेदोऽथर्ववेदः शिक्षा कल्पो व्याकरणं निरुक्तं छन्दो ज्योतिषमिति | अथ परा यया तदक्षरमधिगम्यते ||१.१.५||
verses.transliteration: tatrāparā ṛgvedo yajurvedaḥ sāmavedo'tharvavedaḥ śikṣā kalpo vyākaraṇaṃ niruktaṃ chando jyotiṣam iti | atha parā yayā tad akṣaram adhigamyate ||1.1.5||

word_meanings.meaning: by which the Imperishable is known

verses.english_translation: Of these, the lower is the Rigveda, Yajurveda, Samaveda, Atharvaveda, phonetics, ritual, grammar, etymology, metre, and astronomy. And the higher is that by which the Imperishable is known.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपरा — चतुर्वेदाः षडङ्गानि च। परा — ब्रह्मज्ञानम् — यया अक्षरम् — अविनाशि ब्रह्म — अधिगम्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The lower knowledge comprises the four Vedas and their six auxiliaries. The higher knowledge is that by which the Imperishable Brahman is directly known. The lower leads to worldly and heavenly results; the higher leads to liberation.

verses.sanskrit:
यथा सुदीप्तात् पावकाद्विस्फुलिङ्गाः सहस्रशः प्रभवन्ते सरूपाः |
तथाक्षरात् विविधाः सोम्य भावाः प्रजायन्ते तत्र चैवापि यन्ति ||२.१.१||
verses.transliteration:
yathā sudīptāt pāvakād visphuliṅgāḥ sahasraśaḥ prabhavante sarūpāḥ |
tathākṣarāt vividhāḥ somya bhāvāḥ prajāyante tatra caivāpi yanti ||2.1.1||
verses.english_translation: As from a blazing fire thousands of sparks of the same nature leap forth, so from the Imperishable, O dear one, various beings arise and return there.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सुदीप्तात् पावकात् — प्रज्वलितात् अग्नेः — विस्फुलिङ्गाः — अग्निसरूपाः — प्रभवन्ते। तथा अक्षरात् ब्रह्मणः विविधाः भावाः — जीवाः — प्रजायन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Just as sparks of the same nature as fire arise from a blazing fire, so from the Imperishable Brahman, various beings — individual souls — arise. And just as sparks return to the fire, all beings ultimately return to Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
यथोर्णनाभिः सृजते गृह्णते च यथा पृथिव्यामोषधयः सम्भवन्ति |
यथा सतः पुरुषात्केशलोमानि तथाऽक्षरात्सम्भवतीह विश्वम् ||२.१.७||
verses.transliteration:
yathorṇanābhiḥ sṛjate gṛhṇate ca yathā pṛthivyām oṣadhayaḥ sambhavanti |
yathā sataḥ puruṣāt keśalomāni tathā'kṣarāt sambhavatīha viśvam ||2.1.7||
verses.english_translation: As a spider creates and withdraws, as plants grow from the earth, as hair grows from a living person — so from the Imperishable this universe arises.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ऊर्णनाभिः — मकडः — स्वतः तन्तुम् सृजति गृह्णाति च। तथा अक्षरात् ब्रह्मणः विश्वम् सम्भवति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Three analogies show that the universe arises from Brahman without any external material. The spider creates its web from itself; plants grow from earth; hair grows from the body. Similarly, the universe arises from Brahman alone, without any separate material cause.

verses.sanskrit:
तपति सूर्यो न तपति चन्द्रतारकं नेमा विद्युतो भान्ति कुतोऽयमग्निः |
तमेव भान्तमनुभाति सर्वं तस्य भासा सर्वमिदं विभाति ||२.२.१०||
verses.transliteration:
tapati sūryo na tapati candratārakaṃ nemā vidyuto bhānti kuto'yam agniḥ |
tam eva bhāntam anubhāti sarvaṃ tasya bhāsā sarvam idaṃ vibhāti ||2.2.10||

word_meanings.meaning: does not shine (by its own light)

verses.english_translation: The sun does not shine there, nor the moon and stars, nor these lightnings — how then this fire? Everything shines after that one shines. By its light all this is illumined.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तमेव भान्तम् — ब्रह्म एव प्रकाशमानम् — अनुभाति सर्वम् — सूर्यादयः अनुप्रकाशन्ते। तस्य भासा — ब्रह्मणः प्रकाशेन — सर्वम् विभाति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman alone is self-luminous. The sun, moon, stars, and fire all shine by reflecting Brahman's light. Without Brahman's consciousness illumining them, no light could shine. This verse appears also in the Katha and Bhagavad Gita.

verses.sanskrit:
भिद्यते हृदयग्रन्थिश्छिद्यन्ते सर्वसंशयाः |
क्षीयन्ते चास्य कर्माणि तस्मिन्दृष्टे परावरे ||२.२.८||
verses.transliteration:
bhidyate hṛdayagranthiś chidyante sarvasaṃśayāḥ |
kṣīyante cāsya karmāṇi tasmin dṛṣṭe parāvare ||2.2.8||

word_meanings.meaning: the knot of the heart is cut
when the higher and lower are seen

verses.english_translation: The knot of the heart is cut, all doubts are severed, and all one's karmas are destroyed when the higher and lower are seen.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: हृदयग्रन्थिः — अविद्याकामकर्मरूपः ग्रन्थिः — भिद्यते। सर्वसंशयाः — छिद्यन्ते। कर्माणि — क्षीयन्ते। परावरे — परम् च अवरम् च — ब्रह्म च जीवः च — दृष्टे — एकत्वेन ज्ञाते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The 'knot of the heart' is the knot of ignorance, desire, and karma. When Brahman is directly realized — when the higher (Brahman) and lower (individual self) are seen as one — this knot is cut, all doubts vanish, and all karmas are destroyed. This is liberation.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्म कर्म समुद्भवम् ||३.१.९||
verses.transliteration: brahmaiva tena gantavyaṃ brahma karma samudbhavam ||3.1.9||

word_meanings.meaning: Brahman alone is to be reached

verses.english_translation: Brahman alone is to be reached by one whose actions arise from Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ब्रह्मकर्मसमुद्भवः — यस्य कर्म ब्रह्मणः एव — स ब्रह्म एव गच्छति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: One whose actions arise from the knowledge of Brahman — who acts without ego, as an instrument of Brahman — reaches Brahman alone. Action done in the spirit of Brahman leads back to Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: तमेवं विद्वानमृत इह भवति | नान्यः पन्था अयनाय विद्यते ||३.२.९||
verses.transliteration: tam evaṃ vidvān amṛta iha bhavati | nānyaḥ panthā ayanāya vidyate ||3.2.9||
verses.english_translation: One who knows this thus becomes immortal here itself. There is no other path for liberation.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एवम् विद्वान् — ब्रह्म एवम् जानाति — सः अमृतः इह भवति — जीवन्मुक्तः भवति। नान्यः पन्थाः — ब्रह्मज्ञानात् अन्यः मोक्षमार्गः नास्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: One who knows Brahman as taught becomes immortal even while living (jivanmukta). There is no other path to liberation — not ritual, not austerity, not charity alone. Knowledge of Brahman is the only direct path to liberation.

verses.sanskrit: परीक्ष्य लोकान् कर्मचितान् ब्राह्मणो निर्वेदमायात् | नास्त्यकृतः कृतेन | तद्विज्ञानार्थं स गुरुमेवाभिगच्छेत् ||१.२.१२||
verses.transliteration: parīkṣya lokān karmacitān brāhmaṇo nirvedam āyāt | nāsty akṛtaḥ kṛtena | tad vijñānārthaṃ sa gurum evābhigacchet ||1.2.12||

word_meanings.meaning: having examined the worlds won by karma
the uncreated cannot be reached by the created

verses.english_translation: Having examined the worlds won by karma, let the brahmin come to dispassion. The uncreated cannot be reached by the created. To know That, let him approach a teacher.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: कर्मचितान् लोकान् परीक्ष्य — कर्मफलभोगस्थानानि अनित्यानि इति विचार्य — निर्वेदम् आयात् — वैराग्यम् प्राप्नुयात्। गुरुम् अभिगच्छेत् — श्रोत्रियं ब्रह्मनिष्ठम् गुरुम् उपगच्छेत्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having seen that all worlds won by karma are impermanent, the seeker should develop dispassion (vairagya). The uncreated Brahman cannot be reached by created means (karma). Therefore, one must approach a qualified teacher — one who is learned in scripture and established in Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: तद्विज्ञानार्थं स गुरुमेवाभिगच्छेत् समित्पाणिः श्रोत्रियं ब्रह्मनिष्ठम् ||१.२.१२||
verses.transliteration: tad vijñānārthaṃ sa gurum evābhigacchet samitpāṇiḥ śrotriyaṃ brahmanisṭham ||1.2.12||

word_meanings.meaning: with fuel in hand (as a student)

verses.english_translation: To know That, let him approach a teacher with fuel in hand — one who is learned in the Vedas and established in Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: श्रोत्रियम् — वेदाध्यायिनम्। ब्रह्मनिष्ठम् — ब्रह्मणि निष्ठा यस्य। उभयगुणसम्पन्नः गुरुः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The qualified teacher must have two qualities: shrotiryam (mastery of Vedic scripture) and brahmanishtha (direct establishment in Brahman). A teacher with only scriptural knowledge but no realization, or realization without scriptural grounding, is insufficient.

verses.sanskrit: एते सप्त ज्वलन्ति तनवः | सप्त समिधः | सप्त होमाः | सप्त इमे लोकाः ||१.२.४||
verses.transliteration: ete sapta jvalanti tanavaḥ | sapta samidhaḥ | sapta homāḥ | sapta ime lokāḥ ||1.2.4||
verses.english_translation: These seven flame as tongues; seven are the fuels; seven are the oblations; these seven are the worlds.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सप्त ज्वालाः — काली कराली च मनोजवा च — इत्यादयः। सप्त समिधः — सप्त होमाः — सप्त लोकाः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The seven tongues of fire (Kali, Karali, Manojava, etc.) represent the seven aspects of the sacrificial fire. This section describes the Vedic ritual in detail before showing its limitations compared to the higher knowledge of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
प्लवा ह्येते अदृढा यज्ञरूपा अष्टादशोक्तमवरं येषु कर्म |
एतच्छ्रेयो येऽभिनन्दन्ति मूढा जरामृत्युं ते पुनरेवापि यन्ति ||१.२.७||
verses.transliteration:
plavā hy ete adṛḍhā yajñarūpā aṣṭādaśoktam avaraṃ yeṣu karma |
etac chreyo ye'bhinandanti mūḍhā jarāmṛtyuṃ te punar evāpi yanti ||1.2.7||

word_meanings.meaning: go to old age and death

verses.english_translation: These eighteen forms of sacrifice are unstable rafts. The deluded who consider this the highest good go again to old age and death.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्लवाः — नौकाः — अदृढाः — अस्थिराः। यज्ञाः — संसारसागरे तारकाः न भवन्ति। मूढाः — अज्ञाः — जरामृत्युम् यन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The eighteen limbs of sacrifice are like unstable rafts — they cannot carry one across the ocean of samsara. The deluded who think ritual alone is the highest good continue to be reborn into old age and death. Only knowledge of Brahman leads to liberation.

verses.sanskrit: दिव्यो ह्यमूर्तः पुरुषः सबाह्याभ्यन्तरो ह्यजः | अप्राणो ह्यमनाः शुभ्रो ह्यक्षरात्परतः परः ||२.१.२||
verses.transliteration: divyo hy amūrtaḥ puruṣaḥ sabāhyābhyantaro hy ajaḥ | aprāṇo hy amanāḥ śubhro hy akṣarāt parataḥ paraḥ ||2.1.2||
verses.english_translation: The divine, formless Person is outside and inside, unborn, without prana, without mind, pure, higher than the highest Imperishable.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: दिव्यः — प्रकाशस्वरूपः। अमूर्तः — स्थूलशरीररहितः। सबाह्याभ्यन्तरः — सर्वत्र व्याप्तः। अक्षरात् परतः परः — अव्याकृतादपि उत्कृष्टः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is divine and formless — it is pure light. It is both inside and outside all things. It is unborn, without prana or mind (which are its instruments, not its nature). It is higher than even the Imperishable (akshara) — it is the ultimate ground.

verses.sanskrit:
सत्यमेव जयते नानृतं सत्येन पन्था विततो देवयानः |
येनाक्रमन्त्यृषयो ह्याप्तकामा यत्र तत्सत्यस्य परमं निधानम् ||३.१.६||
verses.transliteration:
satyam eva jayate nānṛtaṃ satyena panthā vitato devayānaḥ |
yenākramanty ṛṣayo hy āptakāmā yatra tat satyasya paramaṃ nidhānam ||3.1.6||

word_meanings.meaning: sages who have attained their desires

verses.english_translation: Truth alone triumphs, not untruth. By truth the divine path is spread — by which the sages who have attained their desires travel to where the supreme treasure of truth abides.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सत्यम् एव जयते — सत्यम् — ब्रह्म — जयते — प्रकाशते। सत्येन पन्थाः — ब्रह्मज्ञानेन मार्गः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Truth (Brahman) alone triumphs — not untruth (the phenomenal world). By truth — by the knowledge of Brahman — the divine path is revealed. The sages who have fulfilled all desires travel this path to the supreme treasure of truth — Brahman itself. This verse is the motto of India.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मविद् ब्रह्मैव भवति | तदेव ब्रह्म ज्योतिषां ज्योतिः | आयुर्होपनिषत् ||३.२.९||
verses.transliteration: brahmavid brahmaiva bhavati | tad eva brahma jyotiṣāṃ jyotiḥ | āyur hopaṇiṣat ||3.2.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the knower of Brahman becomes Brahman itself
this is the Upanishad of life/immortality

verses.english_translation: The knower of Brahman becomes Brahman itself. That Brahman is the light of lights. This is the Upanishad of life.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ब्रह्मवित् — ब्रह्म जानाति — ब्रह्म एव भवति — ब्रह्मैक्यम् प्राप्नोति। ज्योतिषाम् ज्योतिः — सर्वप्रकाशकम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The knower of Brahman becomes Brahman — not metaphorically but literally. The individual self merges into the universal Self. Brahman is the light of all lights — the consciousness that illumines the sun, moon, and all luminaries. This is the concluding teaching of the Mundaka.

verses.sanskrit: शौनको ह वै महाशालोऽङ्गिरसं विधिवदुपसन्नः पप्रच्छ | कस्मिन्नु भगवो विज्ञाते सर्वमिदं विज्ञातं भवतीति ||१.१.३||
verses.transliteration: śaunako ha vai mahāśālo'ṅgirasaṃ vidhivad upasannaḥ papraccha | kasmin nu bhagavo vijñāte sarvam idaṃ vijñātaṃ bhavatīti ||1.1.3||
verses.english_translation: Shaunaka, the great householder, having approached Angiras properly, asked: 'O Blessed One, by knowing what does all this become known?'
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: शौनकः — महाशालः — गृहस्थः — अङ्गिरसम् — विधिवत् उपसन्नः — पप्रच्छ — कस्मिन् विज्ञाते — सर्वम् विज्ञातम् भवति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Shaunaka, a great householder, approached the sage Angiras properly (with humility and gifts) and asked the fundamental question: by knowing what one thing does everything become known? This is the question that opens the Mundaka Upanishad.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मै स होवाच | द्वे विद्ये वेदितव्ये इति ह स्म यद्ब्रह्मविदो वदन्ति परा चैवापरा च ||१.१.४||
verses.transliteration: tasmai sa hovāca | dve vidye veditavye iti ha sma yad brahmavido vadanti parā caivāparā ca ||1.1.4||

word_meanings.meaning: two knowledges are to be known

verses.english_translation: To him he said: 'Two kinds of knowledge are to be known — so say the knowers of Brahman — the higher and the lower.'
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: परा — ब्रह्मज्ञानम्। अपरा — ऋग्वेदादिचतुर्वेदाः सशिक्षाव्याकरणादयश्च।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The higher knowledge is the direct knowledge of Brahman. The lower knowledge comprises the four Vedas and their six auxiliaries. The lower leads to worldly and heavenly results; the higher leads to liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यथा सुदीप्तात्पावकाद्विस्फुलिङ्गाः सहस्रशः प्रभवन्ते सरूपाः | तथाऽक्षरात्सम्भवन्तीह विविधाः सोम्य भावाः ||२.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yathā sudīptāt pāvakād visphuliṅgāḥ sahasraśaḥ prabhavante sarūpāḥ | tathā'kṣarāt sambhavantīha vividhāḥ somya bhāvāḥ ||2.1.1||
verses.english_translation: As from a blazing fire thousands of sparks of the same nature leap forth, so from the Imperishable, O dear one, various beings arise here.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सुदीप्तात् पावकात् — प्रज्वलितात् अग्नेः — विस्फुलिङ्गाः — अग्निसरूपाः — प्रभवन्ते। तथा अक्षरात् ब्रह्मणः विविधाः भावाः — जीवाः — प्रजायन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Just as sparks of the same nature as fire arise from a blazing fire, so from the Imperishable Brahman, various beings arise. The sparks are of the same nature as fire — similarly, all beings are of the same nature as Brahman. This is the teaching of non-duality.

verses.sanskrit: परीक्ष्य लोकान् कर्मचितान् ब्राह्मणो निर्वेदमायात् | नास्त्यकृतः कृतेन ||१.२.१२||
verses.transliteration: parīkṣya lokān karmacitān brāhmaṇo nirvedam āyāt | nāsty akṛtaḥ kṛtena ||1.2.12||

word_meanings.meaning: having examined the worlds won by karma
the uncreated cannot be reached by the created

verses.english_translation: Having examined the worlds won by karma, let the brahmin come to dispassion. The uncreated cannot be reached by the created.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: कर्मचितान् लोकान् — कर्मफलभोगस्थानानि — अनित्यानि — परीक्ष्य — निर्वेदम् आयात्। अकृतः — ब्रह्म — कृतेन — कर्मणा — न प्राप्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having seen that all worlds won by karma are impermanent, the seeker should develop dispassion. The uncreated Brahman cannot be reached by created means (karma). Only knowledge of Brahman leads to liberation.

verses.sanskrit: तमेव विदित्वाऽतिमृत्युमेति | नान्यः पन्था विद्यतेऽयनाय ||३.२.९||
verses.transliteration: tam eva viditvā'timṛtyum eti | nānyaḥ panthā vidyate'yanāya ||3.2.9||
verses.english_translation: Having known that alone, one goes beyond death. There is no other path for liberation.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तम् — ब्रह्म — विदित्वा — अतिमृत्युम् एति — मृत्युम् अतिक्रामति। नान्यः पन्थाः — ब्रह्मज्ञानात् अन्यः मोक्षमार्गः नास्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known Brahman, one transcends death — attains immortality. There is no other path to liberation. Not ritual, not austerity, not charity alone — only the direct knowledge of Brahman leads to liberation. This is the central teaching of the Mundaka.

verses.sanskrit: विश्वतश्चक्षुरुत विश्वतोमुखो विश्वतोबाहुरुत विश्वतस्पात् | सं बाहुभ्यां धमति सं पतत्रैर्द्यावाभूमी जनयन् देव एकः ||२.१.४||
verses.transliteration: viśvataś cakṣur uta viśvatomukho viśvatobāhur uta viśvataspāt | saṃ bāhubhyāṃ dhamati saṃ patatrair dyāvābhūmī janayan deva ekaḥ ||2.1.4||
verses.english_translation: With eyes on all sides, faces on all sides, arms on all sides, feet on all sides — the one God fans with his arms, fans with his wings, creating heaven and earth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: विश्वतः चक्षुः — सर्वत्र दृष्टिः यस्य। विश्वतः मुखः — सर्वत्र मुखम् यस्य। देव एकः — अद्वितीयः परमेश्वरः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman has eyes, faces, arms, and feet on all sides — meaning it pervades all directions and all space. The one God creates heaven and earth. This describes Brahman's omnipresence and creative power.

verses.sanskrit: सत्यमेव जयते नानृतं सत्येन पन्था विततो देवयानः ||३.१.६||
verses.transliteration: satyam eva jayate nānṛtaṃ satyena panthā vitato devayānaḥ ||3.1.6||
verses.english_translation: Truth alone triumphs, not untruth. By truth the divine path is spread — by which the sages travel to where the supreme treasure of truth abides.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सत्यम् — ब्रह्म — जयते। सत्येन — ब्रह्मज्ञानेन — देवयानः पन्थाः — विततः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Truth (Brahman) alone triumphs. By truth — by the knowledge of Brahman — the divine path is revealed. This verse is the national motto of India (Satyameva Jayate) and encapsulates the entire teaching of the Mundaka.

verses.sanskrit: भिद्यते हृदयग्रन्थिश्छिद्यन्ते सर्वसंशयाः | क्षीयन्ते चास्य कर्माणि तस्मिन्दृष्टे परावरे ||२.२.८||
verses.transliteration: bhidyate hṛdayagranthiś chidyante sarvasaṃśayāḥ | kṣīyante cāsya karmāṇi tasmin dṛṣṭe parāvare ||2.2.8||

word_meanings.meaning: the knot of the heart is cut
when the higher and lower are seen

verses.english_translation: The knot of the heart is cut, all doubts are severed, and all one's karmas are destroyed when the higher and lower are seen.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: हृदयग्रन्थिः — अविद्याकामकर्मरूपः — भिद्यते। सर्वसंशयाः — छिद्यन्ते। कर्माणि — क्षीयन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The 'knot of the heart' is the knot of ignorance, desire, and karma. When Brahman is directly realized, this knot is cut, all doubts vanish, and all karmas are destroyed. This is liberation.

verses.sanskrit: हिरण्मये परे कोशे विरजं ब्रह्म निष्कलम् | तच्छुभ्रं ज्योतिषां ज्योतिस्तद्यदात्मविदो विदुः ||२.२.९||
verses.transliteration: hiraṇmaye pare kośe virajaṃ brahma niṣkalam | tac chubhraṃ jyotiṣāṃ jyotis tad yad ātmavido viduḥ ||2.2.9||
verses.english_translation: In the golden supreme sheath is Brahman, stainless, without parts. That is the pure light of lights — that which the knowers of the Self know.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: हिरण्मये कोशे — आनन्दमयकोशे — विरजम् ब्रह्म — निष्कलम् — निरवयवम्। ज्योतिषाम् ज्योतिः — सर्वप्रकाशकम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Within the golden sheath (the anandamaya kosha) is Brahman — stainless, without parts, the pure light of all lights. The knowers of the Self know this Brahman directly. It is the innermost reality, beyond all sheaths.

verses.sanskrit: न चक्षुषा गृह्यते नापि वाचा नान्यैर्देवैस्तपसा कर्मणा वा | ज्ञानप्रसादेन विशुद्धसत्त्वस्ततस्तु तं पश्यते निष्कलं ध्यायमानः ||३.१.८||
verses.transliteration: na cakṣuṣā gṛhyate nāpi vācā nānyair devais tapasā karmaṇā vā | jñānaprasādena viśuddhasattvas tatas tu taṃ paśyate niṣkalaṃ dhyāyamānaḥ ||3.1.8||
verses.english_translation: It is not grasped by the eye, nor by speech, nor by the other senses, nor by austerity or action. By the grace of knowledge, one of purified being, meditating, sees the partless one.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: न चक्षुषा — इन्द्रियैः न गृह्यते। न तपसा — कर्मणा — न प्राप्यते। ज्ञानप्रसादेन — ब्रह्मज्ञानेन — विशुद्धसत्त्वः — पश्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman cannot be grasped by any sense organ, nor attained by austerity or action alone. Only by the grace of knowledge — by the purification of the mind through discrimination and meditation — does one see the partless Brahman directly.

verses.sanskrit: एष आत्मा सर्वभूतेषु गूढोऽत्मा न प्रकाशते | दृश्यते त्वग्र्यया बुद्ध्या सूक्ष्मया सूक्ष्मदर्शिभिः ||३.१.८||
verses.transliteration: eṣa ātmā sarvabhūteṣu gūḍho'tmā na prakāśate | dṛśyate tv agryayā buddhyā sūkṣmayā sūkṣmadarśibhiḥ ||3.1.8||

word_meanings.meaning: by those who see the subtle

verses.english_translation: This Self, hidden in all beings, does not shine forth. But it is seen by those who see the subtle, through a sharp and subtle intellect.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वभूतेषु गूढः — सर्वेषु प्राणिषु अन्तर्हितः। न प्रकाशते — सामान्यजनाय न दृश्यते। अग्र्यया बुद्ध्या — तीक्ष्णया बुद्ध्या — दृश्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self is hidden in all beings but does not reveal itself to ordinary perception. It is seen only by those with a sharp, subtle intellect — those who have purified their minds through discrimination and meditation.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मिन् द्यौः पृथिवी चान्तरिक्षमोतं मनः सह प्राणैश्च सर्वैः | तमेवैकं जानथ आत्मानमन्या वाचो विमुञ्चथामृतस्यैष सेतुः ||२.२.५||
verses.transliteration: yasmin dyauḥ pṛthivī cāntarikṣam otaṃ manaḥ saha prāṇaiś ca sarvaiḥ | tam evaikaṃ jānatha ātmānam anyā vāco vimuñcathāmṛtasyaiṣa setuḥ ||2.2.5||

word_meanings.meaning: know that one as the Self

verses.english_translation: In which heaven, earth, and the atmosphere are woven, along with mind and all the pranas — know that one alone as the Self. Leave aside other words. This is the bridge to immortality.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: यस्मिन् — ब्रह्मणि — द्यौः पृथिवी — ओतम् — प्रोतम्। तम् एकम् — आत्मानम् — जानथ। अमृतस्य सेतुः — मोक्षमार्गः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: In Brahman, heaven, earth, atmosphere, mind, and all pranas are woven together. Know that one Brahman as the Self. Leave aside all other words and teachings — this knowledge alone is the bridge to immortality.

verses.sanskrit: प्राणश्चक्षुः श्रोत्रं खानि वाक् मनश्च | ते प्रक्षीणे काले सम्प्रयान्ति | तान् यथाक्रमं प्रतिष्ठापयन्ति ||२.२.७||
verses.transliteration: prāṇaś cakṣuḥ śrotraṃ khāni vāk manaś ca | te prakṣīṇe kāle samprayānti | tān yathākramaṃ pratiṣṭhāpayanti ||2.2.7||
verses.english_translation: Prana, eye, ear, the openings, speech, and mind — when the time is exhausted, they all depart together.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः — चक्षुः — श्रोत्रम् — वाक् — मनः — प्रक्षीणे काले — सम्प्रयान्ति — मृत्युकाले।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: At the time of death, all the vital forces — prana, sight, hearing, speech, and mind — depart together. They return to their cosmic sources. The Self alone remains, witnessing the departure of all these instruments.

verses.sanskrit: स वेदैतत्परमं ब्रह्म धाम | यत्र विश्वं निहितं भाति शुभ्रम् | उपासते पुरुषं ये ह्यकामास्ते शुक्रमेतदतिवर्तन्ति धीराः ||३.२.१||
verses.transliteration: sa vedaitat paramaṃ brahma dhāma | yatra viśvaṃ nihitaṃ bhāti śubhram | upāsate puruṣaṃ ye hy akāmās te śukram etad ativartanti dhīrāḥ ||3.2.1||

word_meanings.meaning: in which the universe is established

verses.english_translation: He knows this supreme abode of Brahman, in which the universe is established and shines pure. The wise who are free from desire worship the Person — they go beyond this bright one.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: परमम् ब्रह्म धाम — ब्रह्मणः उत्तमम् स्थानम्। अकामाः — इच्छारहिताः — धीराः — शुक्रम् अतिवर्तन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The supreme abode of Brahman is where the entire universe is established and shines pure. The wise who are free from desire go beyond even the bright Brahmaloka — they attain the highest Brahman, beyond all worlds.

verses.sanskrit: तपसा चीयते ब्रह्म ततोऽन्नमभिजायते | अन्नात्प्राणो मनः सत्यं लोकाः कर्मसु चामृतम् ||१.१.८||
verses.transliteration: tapasā cīyate brahma tato'nnam abhijāyate | annāt prāṇo manaḥ satyaṃ lokāḥ karmasu cāmṛtam ||1.1.8||
verses.english_translation: Through austerity Brahman grows; from that food is born; from food, prana; mind; truth; worlds; actions; and immortality.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तपसा — ब्रह्मणः संकल्पेन — अन्नम् — प्राणः — मनः — सत्यम् — लोकाः — क्रमेण उत्पद्यन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Through the austerity (tapas = creative will) of Brahman, the entire chain of creation unfolds: food, prana, mind, truth, worlds, actions, and immortality. This describes the cosmological sequence from Brahman to the manifest world.

verses.sanskrit: यः सर्वज्ञः सर्वविद्यस्यैष महिमा भुवि | दिव्ये ब्रह्मपुरे ह्येष व्योम्न्यात्मा प्रतिष्ठितः ||२.२.७||
verses.transliteration: yaḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvavid yasyaiṣa mahimā bhuvi | divye brahma-pure hy eṣa vyomny ātmā pratiṣṭhitaḥ ||2.2.7||

word_meanings.meaning: in the divine city of Brahman
the Self is established in the ether

verses.english_translation: He who is omniscient, all-knowing, whose glory is on earth — in the divine city of Brahman, the Self is established in the ether of the heart.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वज्ञः — सर्वम् जानाति। दिव्ये ब्रह्मपुरे — हृदयाकाशे — आत्मा प्रतिष्ठितः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The omniscient Brahman is established in the ether of the heart — the 'divine city of Brahman' is the body, and the heart is its innermost chamber where the Self dwells. The Self is both the cosmic omniscient and the intimate inner presence.

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरनेता प्रतिष्ठितोऽन्ने हृदयं सन्निधाय | तद्विज्ञानेन परिपश्यन्ति धीरा आनन्दरूपममृतं यद्विभाति ||२.२.७||
verses.transliteration: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīranetā pratiṣṭhito'nne hṛdayaṃ sannidhāya | tad vijñānena paripaśyanti dhīrā ānandarūpam amṛtaṃ yad vibhāti ||2.2.7||

word_meanings.meaning: made of mind, leader of prana and body
of the form of bliss, immortal

verses.english_translation: Made of mind, leader of prana and body, established in food, dwelling in the heart — the wise perceive through knowledge that which shines as bliss, as immortal.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयः — मनःस्वरूपः। प्राणशरीरनेता — प्राणस्य शरीरस्य च नेता। हृदये — सन्निधाय — आनन्दरूपम् — अमृतम् — विभाति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self is made of mind (consciousness), leads prana and the body, and dwells in the heart. The wise perceive it through knowledge as the shining, blissful, immortal Brahman. This is the direct vision of the Self in the heart.

verses.sanskrit: भिद्यते हृदयग्रन्थिश्छिद्यन्ते सर्वसंशयाः | क्षीयन्ते चास्य कर्माणि तस्मिन्दृष्टे परावरे ||२.२.८||
verses.transliteration: bhidyate hṛdayagranthiś chidyante sarvasaṃśayāḥ | kṣīyante cāsya karmāṇi tasmin dṛṣṭe parāvare ||2.2.8||

word_meanings.meaning: the knot of the heart is cut
when the higher and lower are seen as one

verses.english_translation: The knot of the heart is cut, all doubts are severed, and all karmas are destroyed when the higher and lower are seen as one.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: हृदयग्रन्थिः — अविद्याकामकर्मरूपः — भिद्यते। परावरे दृष्टे — जीवब्रह्मैक्यम् ज्ञात्वा।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: When the identity of the individual self (avara) and Brahman (para) is directly realized, the knot of ignorance is cut, all doubts vanish, and all karmas are destroyed. This is the most celebrated verse of the Mundaka — the description of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: स वेदैतत्परमं ब्रह्म धाम | यत्र विश्वं निहितं भाति शुभ्रम् | उपासते पुरुषं ये ह्यकामास्ते शुक्रमेतदतिवर्तन्ति धीराः ||३.२.१||
verses.transliteration: sa vedaitat paramaṃ brahma dhāma | yatra viśvaṃ nihitaṃ bhāti śubhram | upāsate puruṣaṃ ye hy akāmās te śukram etad ativartanti dhīrāḥ ||3.2.1||

word_meanings.meaning: in which the universe is established
the wise who are free from desire

verses.english_translation: He knows this supreme abode of Brahman, in which the universe is established and shines pure. The wise who are free from desire go beyond this bright one.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: परमम् ब्रह्म धाम — ब्रह्मणः उत्तमम् स्थानम्। अकामाः — इच्छारहिताः — धीराः — शुक्रम् अतिवर्तन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The supreme abode of Brahman is where the entire universe is established and shines pure. The wise who are free from desire go beyond even the bright Brahmaloka — they attain the highest Brahman, beyond all worlds.

verses.sanskrit: कामान् यः कामयते मन्यमानः स कर्मभिर्जायते तत्र तत्र | पूर्णस्य पूर्णमादाय पूर्णमेवावशिष्यते ||३.२.२||
verses.transliteration: kāmān yaḥ kāmayate manyamānaḥ sa karmabhir jāyate tatra tatra | pūrṇasya pūrṇam ādāya pūrṇam evāvaśiṣyate ||3.2.2||

word_meanings.meaning: taking the full from the full

verses.english_translation: One who desires desires is born through karmas here and there. Taking the full from the full, the full alone remains.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: कामान् कामयते — इच्छाः इच्छति — कर्मभिः जायते। पूर्णस्य पूर्णम् आदाय — ब्रह्मणः जगत् उत्पन्नम् — ब्रह्म पूर्णम् एव।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: One who desires continues to be reborn through karma. But Brahman is the fullness — taking the full (the world) from the full (Brahman), the full (Brahman) alone remains. This famous verse from the Isha Upanishad is echoed here — Brahman's fullness is never diminished by creation.

verses.sanskrit: स य एवं विद्वान् ब्रह्मविद् ब्रह्मैव भवति | नास्यादहीयसे गोत्रे जायते | स तरति शोकं तरति पाप्मानम् | गुहाग्रन्थिभ्यो विमुक्तोऽमृतो भवति ||३.२.९||
verses.transliteration: sa ya evaṃ vidvān brahmavid brahmaiva bhavati | nāsyādahīyase gotre jāyate | sa tarati śokaṃ tarati pāpmānam | guhāgranthibhyo vimukto'mṛto bhavati ||3.2.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the knower of Brahman becomes Brahman
is not born in the lineage
freed from the knots of the cave

verses.english_translation: One who knows thus — the knower of Brahman becomes Brahman itself. He is not born in the lineage. He crosses grief, crosses sin, freed from the knots of the cave, he becomes immortal.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ब्रह्मवित् — ब्रह्म एव भवति — ब्रह्मैक्यम् प्राप्नोति। शोकम् तरति — पाप्मानम् तरति — अमृतः भवति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The knower of Brahman becomes Brahman — not metaphorically but literally. He crosses grief and sin, is freed from the knots of the heart (the cave), and becomes immortal. This is the final teaching of the Mundaka — the complete description of liberation.
